codeReview.summary: Reviewing the original failed diff and this rerun fix shows the new commit improves the old fingerprint-only remapping, but it still does not fully resolve the original scrutiny failure. The tracker now assigns ephemeral source IDs to prepared items, yet `register_filtered_input_sources()` can only reuse them when the filtered item still carries the same heap identity as the prepared item or the same serialized fingerprint. If a filter drops or reorders siblings and rewrites another item by replacing it with a fresh `InputItem`, the function still falls through to `unwrap_or(0)` and guesses the first remaining source, so sent-state can drift and the actually delivered source can replay later.

issues.description: `register_filtered_input_sources()` still guesses source identity when a filtered rewrite is expressed as a fresh replacement instead of an in-place mutation. The new identity path relies on `input_item_identity()` (`crates/agents-core/src/internal/oai_conversation.rs:364-382`), which is derived from heap addresses of the current text/JSON storage; a replaced filtered item therefore has neither the original identity nor the original fingerprint, so the code falls back to `.unwrap_or(0)` (`crates/agents-core/src/internal/oai_conversation.rs:160-175`) and attributes it to the first remaining prepared source. The public filter API allows exactly this replacement style (`crates/agents-core/src/run.rs:2872-2876`), and the added regression only covers an in-place JSON mutation (`crates/agents-core/src/internal/oai_conversation.rs:453-486`), not a replacement rewrite combined with drop/reorder. In that mixed case the wrong source can still be marked sent and the real delivered source can replay on a later server-managed turn, so the original scrutiny failure remains unresolved. The JS reference avoids this by carrying explicit per-filtered-item `sourceItems` mappings instead of guessing from filtered order (`reference/openai-agents-js/packages/agents-core/src/runner/conversation.ts:38-49,135-169,370-431`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission docs capture the invariant that server-managed conversation tracking must preserve source attribution across `call_model_input_filter` rewrites, but they do not record the concrete Rust/JS API mismatch that makes this bug family recur: Rust `ModelInputData` has no `sourceItems`/`filterApplied` provenance channel, so workers keep trying tracker-side heuristics instead of carrying explicit per-filtered-item source mappings.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/architecture.md` documents the attribution invariant, but `crates/agents-core/src/run_config.rs:23-29` only exposes `{ input, instructions }` while the JS reference returns `sourceItems` and `filterApplied` for filtered input (`reference/openai-agents-js/packages/agents-core/src/runner/conversation.ts:38-49,135-169`).

summary: Fail. The rerun fixes the narrow in-place mutation case, but it still guesses source identity when a dropped/reordered filtered rewrite is emitted as a fresh replacement item, so sent-state can still drift and replay an already-delivered delta later.
